title: What Antibodies Actually Do
script_summary: Explore the crucial role of antibodies in our immune system.

segments.voiceover: Antibodies are Y-shaped proteins that tag threats for your immune system.
But what exactly do they do?
When a virus or bacteria invades, antibodies spring into action.
They bind to the invaders, marking them for destruction.
This helps our immune cells identify and eliminate them.
Think of antibodies as the 'CSI agents' of your body, uncovering and tagging threats.
Curious about your immune system? Hit follow for more insights!
